faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Millburn home?
faqs.answer: Signs include small holes in woolens, cashmere, or silk garments, silky webbing in closet corners, tiny cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or stored blankets. Our Millburn technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What determines the cost of clothes moth control in Millburn?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550-$5,000 depends on the severity of infestation, the number of rooms affected, home size, and treatment method required. A single closet treatment costs significantly less than whole-home fumigation. We provide detailed estimates after inspecting your Millburn property and assessing the scope of moth activity.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and fabrics?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products specifically designed for textile pests that are safe around people and pets when applied correctly. Our treatments target moths without damaging delicate fabrics, and we provide detailed pre-treatment and post-treatment instructions to protect your valuable clothing and household textiles.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Millburn home?
faqs.answer: Regular vacuuming of closets, proper storage of seasonal clothing in sealed containers, periodic dry cleaning of woolens, and maintaining low humidity levels all help prevent re-infestation. We provide Millburn homeowners with comprehensive prevention strategies and offer ongoing monitoring services to ensure your home remains moth-free year-round.
